title: Osteological preparation protocol by enzymatic maceration
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"detg","text":"The following protocol is part of my Master's thesis in Archaeology and Archaeology Sciences speciality in Quaternary, Prehistory, and Paleontology.\nIt involves cleaning bone remains from experimental archaeology for the purpose of preservation and the study of taphonomic traces.\nThe goal is to obtain defatted hard animal remains composed solely of connective tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"4qsst","text":"The amount of pancreatin used was 1g and 2g, the temperatures tested were 35°, 40°C and 45°C and the experiment was repeated twice for each combination (N=12). Here the enzymes are in 0.4g capsules we removed them from their capsules.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"14tic","text":"It is necessary to locate the experimental marks before beginning.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"62f9e","text":"The bones should be at room temperature.\nIf there is any soft tissue that can be detached without damaging the rest, it should be removed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"et9a4","text":"The smell of decomposition is strong, we can advise you to put Vivks Vaporub ointment near your nose and wear personal protective equipment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text:
{"blocks":[{"key":"42rqp","text":"- [PPE:] gloves, mask, safety glasses, apron/gown.
- Scalpel
- Pliers
- Plastic or stainless steel container suitable for the remainder
- Oven (here 60x55x35cm with two shelves separated by 24cm)
- Sorbonne
- Pancreatin","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Carefully remove superficial soft tissue with a scalpel, in order to avoid leaving parasitic traces that could be interpreted as experimental.
Dry under the sorbonne as much as necessary.
Place the bones in the containers and cover them with warm water.
Check every 6 hours, if necessary remove the supernatant and suspended parts, in order to remove the degradable materials available to the enzymes already separated from the bone residue.
Add 1g of enzymes to six of the containers and 2g of enzymes to the other six.
Place two containers of 1g of enzymes and two containers of 2g of enzymes in the sorbonne at 35°C.
Repeat the previous steps at 40°C and 45°C.
Defrost bone remains in a sorbonne if they are not at room temperature.
Cover the containers with cling film to prevent water from evaporating.
creator: Anaïs Cornu
